chunk_summary: This section outlines India-specific and international crosswalks for managing pediatric metabolic risks, including obesity, hypertension, MASLD, and youth-onset T2DM, emphasizing when to prioritize local versus global guidelines.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between India-specific and international guidelines for pediatric metabolic risk management.
Understand the application of IAP recommendations for obesity, hypertension, MASLD, and youth-onset T2DM in Indian settings.
Identify gaps in local resources and adapt international guidance appropriately.
Apply evidence-based strategies for pediatric metabolic risk care in resource-limited settings.
key_facts: India-specific IAP guidelines for obesity include growth charts and adult-equivalent cutoffs.
Hypertension management in India aligns with IAP thresholds, with ABPM recommended when available.
MASLD 2024 guidelines in India recommend ALT and ultrasound for diagnosis, with escalation triggers defined.
Youth-onset T2DM care in India follows IAP diagnostic standards, with ISPAD schedules for annual complication screening as a fallback.
International guidelines provide detailed protocols for areas like ambulatory BP monitoring and pediatric liver ultrasound limitations.
reference_quote: India has dedicated IAP recommendations (India-appropriate growth charts and adult-equivalent cutoffs).
document_key_topics: Obesity and hypertension management in children
Crosswalk between local and international guidance
